mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening aggregate demand and rising labor market slack, which compresses near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in >60% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero information coefficient when backtested against realized 60-day returns across three distinct business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding the 102 index threshold signals high capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, supporting corporate margin expansion and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean difference when INDPRO crosses above 102 compared to periods below that threshold (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces market pricing of tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The inflation threshold signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and employment growth signal fails to generate a positive predictive rank correlation with 60-day forward returns across two full economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month employment growth supports consumer spending and corporate cash flows, generating positive short-term equity returns regardless of the prevailing macroeconomic regime.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative or zero information coefficient when backtested against realized 5-day returns across three distinct business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1400 units signals a credit or demand shock that transmits to broader economic activity, triggering earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative mean difference when HOUST drops below 1400 compared to stable periods (p > 0.05).
